# modeldiag

The goal of modeldiag is to provide comprehensive diagnostic checks for
statistical models including linear models, generalized linear models,
and survival models.

## Installation

You can install the development version of modeldiag from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r

# install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("Teniola17/modeldiag")
```

## Example

This is a basic example which shows you how to diagnose a linear model:

``` r

library(modeldiag)

# Fit a linear model
model <- lm(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = mtcars)

# Run diagnostics
diagnostics <- diagnose_model(model)

# View summary
summary(diagnostics)

# Plot diagnostics
plot(diagnostics)
```

## Supported Models

The package currently supports:

- **Linear models** (`lm`): Tests for multicollinearity,
  heteroscedasticity, autocorrelation, normality, and outliers
- **Generalized linear models** (`glm`):
  - Binomial family: Tests for linearity of logit, goodness of fit,
    influential observations, and separation
  - Poisson family: Tests for overdispersion, zero-inflation, and
    residual analysis
- **Cox proportional hazards models** (`coxph`): Tests for proportional
  hazards assumption, influential observations, and functional form

## Features

### Diagnostic Tests

Each model type has specific diagnostic tests:

#### Linear Models

- Variance Inflation Factor (VIF) for multicollinearity
- Breusch-Pagan test for heteroscedasticity\
- Durbin-Watson test for autocorrelation
- Shapiro-Wilk test for normality of residuals
- Cook’s distance for influential observations

#### Logistic Regression

- VIF for multicollinearity
- Box-Tidwell test for linearity of logit
- Hosmer-Lemeshow test for goodness of fit
- Complete/quasi-complete separation detection
- Cook’s distance for influential observations

#### Poisson Regression

- VIF for multicollinearity
- Overdispersion test
- Zero-inflation test
- Residual analysis
- Cook’s distance for influential observations

#### Cox Models

- VIF for multicollinearity (with warnings)
- Schoenfeld residuals test for proportional hazards
- dfbetas for influential observations
- Guidance for functional form assessment

### Visualization

The [`plot()`](https://rdrr.io/r/graphics/plot.default.html) method
provides model-specific diagnostic plots:

- Residuals vs Fitted values
- Q-Q plots
- Scale-Location plots
- Cook’s distance plots
- ACF plots for time series residuals (linear models)
- Schoenfeld residual plots (Cox models)
- dfbeta plots (Cox models)
- Martingale residual plots (Cox models)

## Getting Help

If you encounter a bug, please file an issue with a minimal reproducible
example on [GitHub](https://github.com/Teniola17/modeldiag/issues).#

# Package index

## Main diagnostic workflow

- [`diagnose_model()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/diagnose_model.md)
  : Diagnose Statistical Models

## Linear model checks

- [`check_vif()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_vif.md)
  : Check Variance Inflation Factors
- [`check_heteroskedasticity()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_heteroskedasticity.md)
  : Check Heteroskedasticity
- [`check_autocorrelation()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_autocorrelation.md)
  : Check Autocorrelation
- [`check_linearity()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_linearity.md)
  : Check Linearity
- [`check_normality()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_normality.md)
  : Check Normality of Residuals
- [`check_outliers()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_outliers.md)
  : Check Influential Observations

## Logistic regression checks

- [`check_box_tidwell()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_box_tidwell.md)
  : Check Logistic Linearity with Box-Tidwell Test
- [`check_hosmer_lemeshow()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_hosmer_lemeshow.md)
  : Check Logistic Goodness of Fit
- [`check_influential_glm()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_influential_glm.md)
  : Check Influential Observations in GLMs
- [`check_separation()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_separation.md)
  : Check Separation in GLMs

## Poisson regression checks

- [`check_overdispersion()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_overdispersion.md)
  : Check Overdispersion
- [`check_zero_inflation()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_zero_inflation.md)
  : Check Zero Inflation
- [`check_residual_analysis()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_residual_analysis.md)
  : Check Residual Summary

## Survival model checks

- [`check_proportional_hazards()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_proportional_hazards.md)
  : Check Proportional Hazards
- [`check_influential_coxph()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_influential_coxph.md)
  : Check Influential Observations in Cox Models
- [`check_functional_form_coxph()`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/check_functional_form_coxph.md)
  : Check Cox Model Functional Form

## Output helpers

- [`print(`*`<model_diagnostics>`*`)`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/print.model_diagnostics.md)
  : Print Model Diagnostics
- [`summary(`*`<model_diagnostics>`*`)`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/summary.model_diagnostics.md)
  : Summarize Model Diagnostics
- [`plot(`*`<model_diagnostics>`*`)`](https://teniola17.github.io/modeldiag/reference/plot.model_diagnostics.md)
  : Plot Model Diagnostics
